claim_boundary: Tier 7.7b scores frozen v2.5 on the pre-registered Tier 7.7a standardized scoreboard. It may support standardized progress or localized usefulness, but it does not freeze a new baseline, does not authorize hardware/native transfer, does not tune benchmarks after seeing results, and does not claim language, broad reasoning, AGI, or ASI.

nonclaims: not evidence that planning helps every standardized task
not ESN/ridge/GRU superiority unless the table says so
not language, broad planning, AGI, or ASI evidence
secondary C-MAPSS/NAB evidence cannot rescue a failed primary standardized core
classification.recommendation: Standardized progress versus v2.3 is supported, but external baseline gap remains; do not claim broad superiority.

omitted_contract_baselines.reason: optional in 7.7a if runtime/dependency budget allows; no locked implementation exists in prior standard-scoreboard harness

secondary_public_confirmation.rule: secondary rows are reported only; they cannot rescue failed standardized core

leakage_audit.small_gru_status: omitted: no locked local dependency/runtime budget in prior Tier 7.0 scorer; ESN/ridge/lag/persistence/online LMS retained

diagnostics.role: v2.4 policy/action reference without v2.5 planning columns

diagnostics.ablation: remove v2.5 planning/subgoal meta-state; equals v2.3 reference on standardized core
remove replay-stabilized slow context column; standardized core has no explicit replay buffer

diagnostics.control: candidate readout updates against shuffled training targets
candidate feature rows shuffled within train/test splits
